skill.name: agent-evaluation
skill.description: Testing and benchmarking LLM agents including behavioral testing, capability assessment, reliability metrics, and production monitoring—where even top agents achieve less than 50% on real tasks
skill.summary: Comprehensive LLM agent evaluation with behavioral testing, capability assessments, and reliability metrics for production-ready AI systems
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings determined to be false positives. The external_commands pattern matches markdown backtick formatting for inline code references, not shell execution. The unicode escape sequence is a standard em-dash character in the description. No weak cryptography exists—this is a documentation file with no executable code. The skill describes LLM agent evaluation methodologies and contains no security risks.

content.user_title: Evaluate and Test LLM Agent Performance
content.value_statement: LLM agents often fail in production despite passing benchmarks. This skill provides behavioral testing, capability assessments, and reliability metrics to catch issues before deployment.
actual_capabilities: Design statistical test suites that run multiple times to analyze result distributions
Create behavioral contract tests that verify agent invariants across different inputs
Implement adversarial testing to identify edge cases and failure modes
Build reliability metrics that track agent performance consistency over time
Detect and handle flaky tests inherent to LLM-based systems
Prevent data leakage between test data and training or prompt contexts
limitations: Requires understanding of testing fundamentals and LLM behavior patterns
Cannot guarantee 100% test pass rate due to inherent LLM output variability
Statistical evaluation requires multiple test runs for meaningful results
Does not provide automated test generation—tests must be designed by humans

use_cases.title: Pre-Production Agent Validation
use_cases.description: Run comprehensive behavioral tests on agents before deploying to production environments to catch regressions and capability gaps.

use_cases.title: Agent Comparison and Selection
use_cases.description: Evaluate multiple agent configurations or models against standardized benchmarks to select the best performer for specific tasks.

use_cases.title: Continuous Agent Monitoring
use_cases.description: Implement ongoing reliability metrics and regression tests to detect performance degradation in deployed agents.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Agent Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Test this agent on a simple task and verify the output matches expected behavior. Run the test 3 times and report any inconsistencies.

prompt_templates.title: Behavioral Contract Definition
prompt_templates.prompt: Define behavioral invariants that this agent must maintain across all inputs. Create test cases that verify each invariant holds true.

prompt_templates.title: Adversarial Test Suite
prompt_templates.prompt: Design edge cases and adversarial inputs that could break this agent. Include malformed inputs, ambiguous requests, and conflicting constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Statistical Reliability Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Run this agent on the same task 10 times. Analyze the distribution of outputs, calculate consistency metrics, and identify failure patterns.

output_examples.input: Run behavioral contract test on customer support agent
output_examples.output: Test Results: 5/5 invariants passed across 20 test runs. Consistency score: 94%. Minor variance detected in response tone under high-load scenarios.

output_examples.input: Adversarial testing for code generation agent
output_examples.output: Identified 3 failure modes: (1) Silent failure on malformed syntax, (2) Over-confident incorrect answers on ambiguous specs, (3) Resource exhaustion on recursive tasks.

best_practices: Run tests multiple times and analyze statistical distributions rather than single outcomes
Focus on behavioral invariants rather than exact output string matching
Include adversarial inputs that actively try to break the agent
anti_patterns: Testing agents with single runs—LLM outputs vary and require statistical analysis
Only testing happy paths—edge cases reveal critical failure modes
Optimizing agents for specific metrics rather than actual task performance

faq.question: Why do agents pass benchmarks but fail in production?
faq.answer: Benchmarks often use clean, well-defined tasks while production involves ambiguous, real-world scenarios. This skill bridges that gap with behavioral testing that mirrors actual use cases.

faq.question: How many times should I run each test?
faq.answer: Minimum 3-5 runs for basic tests, 10+ for statistical reliability analysis. More runs provide better confidence in consistency metrics but increase evaluation time.

faq.question: What is a behavioral contract?
faq.answer: A behavioral contract defines invariants the agent must maintain—such as never exposing sensitive data, always asking clarifying questions for ambiguous requests, or maintaining consistent tone across sessions.

faq.question: Can this skill test any type of LLM agent?
faq.answer: Yes, the evaluation methodologies apply to conversational agents, code generation agents, task automation agents, and multi-agent systems. Test design must match the agent's domain.

faq.question: How do I handle flaky tests?
faq.answer: Accept that some variability is inherent to LLMs. Use statistical thresholds (e.g., 90% pass rate) rather than requiring 100% consistency. Track flakiness as a metric itself.

faq.question: What is data leakage in agent evaluation?
faq.answer: Data leakage occurs when test data appears in training data or prompts, causing artificially inflated scores. Always verify test inputs are independent from any data the agent has seen.
